text.title: IndieHackers Build-in-Public Strategy Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding IndieHackers Build-in-Public Strategy. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override.

rule.statement: First 2-4 weeks: replies + advice + zero self-promotion. Builds trust + name recognition.
rule.rationale: Day-1 self-promo posts get downvoted + the founder labeled spammer.

rule.statement: Monthly post with revenue numbers, churn, biggest lesson. Skipping months collapses the flywheel.
rule.rationale: Build-in-public benefits collapse when cadence breaks.

rule.statement: Posts include both wins AND losses. Hype-only content gets ignored.
rule.rationale: Hype-only posts perform 40% worse than mixed.

rule.statement: Reply to ≥3 questions/comments per own post within 24h; otherwise post dies.
After 12 weeks evaluate: trial signups, mailing list growth, replies-per-post. If KPIs flat, refactor or stop.
rule.rationale: Indefinite community-marketing without KPI gates = sunk-cost.

rule.statement: If preconditions in AGENTS.md "Applies If" do not all hold, OR any "Skip If" disqualifier is true, do not run this methodology — route to the named alternative.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are pre-condition gated; running them out-of-scope produces noise and burns the agent context budget.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold, execute the procedure in `content/04-procedure.xml` (or the rule set above when complexity is light), emit the artefact specified by `content/02-output-contract.xml`, and validate with `scripts/validate-growth-indiehackers-strategy.py` before returning.
rule.rationale: Tying the entry rule to the validator script closes the loop between policy and machine check.
